titleStmt.title: SII 19.216: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 216. (A.R. No. 436 of 1904.) VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT. ON A PILLAR IN THE MAṆḌAPA IN FRONT OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, VĒDĀRAṆYĒŚVARA TEMPLE.

summary.p: This records a gift of 90 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumaṟaikkāḍuḍaiyār on behalf of Śēnaṉ alias Maratakaśeṭṭi a merchant of Karuvūr (the present Karūr), by the community Tigai (Diśai) Āyirattaiññūṟṟuvar. The origin of this body is not clear. As its name implies, it must have comprised a membership of 1500 individuals or families. Karuvūr is mentioned frequently in inscriptions, the earliest reference being found in the Brāhmī record from Pugalūr of about the 3rd century B.C. (A.R. No. 343 of 1928).

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . koppara
kecaripaṟmaṟku yāṇṭu
9Āvatu tirumaṟaikkā
ṭuṭaiyāṟkku kiḻ
ccik karuvūrirukkum viyā
pāri ceṉanṉāna marataka
ceṭṭiyaic cāttit
tikaiyāyirattaiñ
ñūṟṟuvar vaippit
ta tirunontaāviḷakku 1
Oṉṟukku vaitta Āṭu
toṇṇūṟuṅ koṇ
ṭu cantirātittaval ni
catam Uḻakku ney yaṭṭu
vomāṉon tevar
maṉṟāṭikaḷom Itu
panmāyeśvarar rirakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 19.216 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
